Set up a Python environment with TensorFlow 2.x installed (e.g., TensorFlow 2.10) and Keras compatible with TensorFlow 2.x
Clone or download the Mask_RCNN repository from https://github.com/matterport/Mask_RCNN or have the same codebase with model.py, parallel_model.py, and config.py available
Ensure h5py is installed in the environment since it is required for loading weights
Import necessary modules: tensorflow as tf, keras, numpy as np, and the MaskRCNN class from modellib (e.g., from mrcnn import model as modellib, from mrcnn.config import Config)

Define or load a Config subclass instance with the following key parameters: IMAGE_SHAPE = [1024, 1024, 3], BATCH_SIZE = 1 (assuming GPU_COUNT=1 and IMAGES_PER_GPU=1), NUM_CLASSES >= 1, IMAGE_META_SIZE computed properly, IMAGE_RESIZE_MODE='square', and other default parameters consistent with the original config
Set MODEL_DIR to a valid directory path for model logs and weights
Set COCO_MODEL_PATH to the path of a Mask R-CNN pre-trained weights file in HDF5 (.h5) format, e.g., 'mask_rcnn_coco.h5', that uses the HDF5 format for weights (not TensorFlow SavedModel format)
Instantiate the MaskRCNN model with mode='inference', config=config instance, and model_dir=MODEL_DIR, i.e., model = modellib.MaskRCNN(mode='inference', model_dir=MODEL_DIR, config=config)
Call model.load_weights(COCO_MODEL_PATH, by_name=True) to load the COCO pre-trained weights
Assert that NotImplementedError is raised with message containing 'Save or restore weights that is not an instance of `tf.Variable` is not supported in h5, use `save_format='tf'` instead'
Verify the traceback points to tensorflow/python/keras/saving/hdf5_format.py line around 895 in _legacy_weights function during load_weights_from_hdf5_group_by_name call
Confirm that the error occurs because the model's layers contain KerasVariable objects instead of tf.Variable instances, triggering the NotImplementedError
Optionally, print the type of weights in one of the Conv2D layers before the weights loading to verify they are KerasVariable objects, not tf.Variable
Validate that the code does not raise the error if the model is instantiated in 'training' mode or if weights are loaded using TensorFlow SavedModel format instead of HDF5
Ensure batch size is 1 and input image dimensions are consistent with config.IMAGE_SHAPE (e.g., 1024x1024x3) to avoid unrelated errors
Do not modify or patch the load_weights method or the underlying keras saving utilities; reproduce the bug with the original code as is
